current_implemented_build: V59 Internet Trust Engine MVP Scaffold
patch_type: Internet Trust Engine scenario-family scaffold over V58.2 queue-to-proof trace linkage
release_chain_status: V59 is the current implemented build. It loads after V58.2 in src/ui/v52-7-operational-layer.js. It introduces the Internet Trust Engine as a bounded scenario family and prototype scaffold inside existing workspaces only. It does not perform live internet retrieval, live evidence retrieval, live claim extraction, live scoring, fact-checker branding, truth-engine branding, or political validation. GitHub Pages browser QA is required after deploy.
top_level_tab_rule: Do not add more top-level tabs. Upgrade existing workspaces only.
version_label_rule: Build and version labels belong in Settings/admin metadata and repo documentation only. They must not appear as executive-facing dashboard content.
public_naming_rule: Public product name is CyberShield Executive OS. Current prototype build is V59. Do not call the public build CyberShield OS v8 unless the repo, README, loader, Settings/admin metadata, and public UX intentionally move to that version scheme.
controlling_doctrine: CyberShield does not ask whether we trust a person. It asks which claims, artifacts, evidence paths, and reliance decisions can be trusted, at what confidence, for what purpose, and with what risk if wrong.

prototype_boundary: The current GitHub Pages build is a lightweight static advisory prototype scaffold. It is not connected to live enterprise telemetry, SIEM, EDR, IAM, Microsoft 365, cloud control planes, GRC tooling, production agent enforcement, Google Sheets, CRM sync, ticketing, notifications, marketplace systems, ad platforms, domain-intelligence systems, identity verification systems, CMMC certification systems, healthcare compliance validation systems, banking/payment systems, live monitoring, live internet retrieval, live evidence retrieval, live scoring, live claim extraction, internet claim verification, report delivery, ticketing, workflow automation, notifications, or takedown automation.
known_limitations: Internet Trust Engine is a scaffold, not a live artifact-analysis engine
V59 does not retrieve internet content
V59 does not retrieve live evidence
V59 does not perform live claim extraction
V59 does not perform live scoring
V59 does not perform political validation
V59 does not label people or sources as trusted/untrusted
V59 does not make legal or compliance determinations
V59 download is a text file, not a branded PDF or board deck
GitHub Pages browser QA required after deploy
